text.title: xUnit Testing Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real xUnit test suites. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Test class uses `[OneTimeSetUp]`/`[TestInitialize]` equivalents copied from MSTest; xUnit silently ignores them; tests fail intermittently.
antipattern.root-cause: Author ported from MSTest / NUnit without learning xUnit's per-test-instance lifecycle.
antipattern.fix: Move shared expensive setup to `IClassFixture<T>`; per-test setup goes in the test class constructor.

antipattern.symptom: CI build flakes on async assertions; local runs pass; one in twenty CI builds fails.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used `Thread.Sleep(2000)` to "wait" for async work to finish.
antipattern.fix: Use polling helpers (`await Awaiter.WaitUntil(...)`) with a tight interval and a generous timeout, or inject a `TimeProvider` and advance time deterministically.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass individually, fail when run in a specific order; root cause hides for weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: `IClassFixture` instance shared across tests carries mutable state (counter, list); writes from one test leak into another.
antipattern.fix: Make fixtures immutable; if shared mutable state is necessary, use `ICollectionFixture` and explicit reset hooks; mark suite `[Collection("...")]` to serialise.

antipattern.symptom: Test failure message: `Expected: List<Order>[3] Actual: List<Order>[3]` — no clue which element differs.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `actual.Should().BeEquivalentTo(expected, opt => opt.WithStrictOrdering())`. The diff identifies the differing element + field.

antipattern.symptom: Tests green on `UseInMemoryDatabase`; prod query against Postgres fails: `operator does not exist: text || jsonb`.

root-cause.source: EF Core docs — InMemory provider warning

antipattern.root-cause: InMemory provider lacks Postgres semantics (JSONB, advisory locks, case sensitivity).
antipattern.fix: Use `Testcontainers.PostgreSql` for data-layer tests; share one container per test collection via `ICollectionFixture`.
